body_txt: Infraction Distraction is when a conspicuous but minor infraction — possibly not even illegal but merely disreputable — is made up or committed for the purposes of distracting authorities from something much more serious. (Usually a crime, but not always; a character can pretend to be distilling moonshine to hide that he's training with his new superpowers, for instance.) Not unlike a Kansas City Shuffle , the guards in question know they're being tricked, but are wrong about what the trick is. If you confess (whether or not you actually committed it), it's Confess to a Lesser Crime , but you can also manufacture evidence against yourself or otherwise plant clues to the infraction, down to simply committing it. One common method is to plant an Incredibly Obvious Tail — to distract from the skilled and subtle tail. Incredibly Obvious Bug can also be used, but is less common. Overlaps with Embarrassing Cover-Up if the decoy secret is disreputable as well as prohibited (or if the character is so Lawful Good or such a Slave to PR that being caught breaking even minor rules is an embarrassment in and of itself). Overlap with We Need a Distraction is possible but rare, since it usually is not to enable further actions. A Smokescreen Crime is when a major crime is used to cover for another major crime. If a suspect is surprisingly quick to confess their Red Herring Secret , there's a good chance it's this. See also Hidden in Plain Sight . A Censor Decoy is this used in Real Life to get crap past the radar . The opposite is Minor Crime Reveals Major Plot .
